methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The contract must always hold enough TITN to cover all claimable balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract's TITN balance falls below totalTitnClaimable (e.g., via admin withdrawal or math error), users will be unable to claim their converted tokens, resulting in insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_solvency() public view { assert(titn.balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalTitnClaimable); }

invariants.description: The sum of total claimable and total claimed TITN must never exceed the maximum allocated TITN_ARB.
invariants.attack_scenario: A flaw in the quote calculation or deposit logic could inflate the claimable amounts, allowing users to claim more TITN than the protocol's hard cap, draining the contract.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_max_titn_cap() public view { assert(totalTitnClaimable + totalTitnClaimed <= TITN_ARB); }

invariants.description: The global totalTitnClaimable must exactly equal the sum of all individual claimableTitnPerUser balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the global accumulator desynchronizes from individual balances, the proportional distribution math in withdrawRemainingTitn will be corrupted, leading to trapped funds or insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claimable_sync() public view { assert(totalTitnClaimable == sum_all_claimable()); }

invariants.description: The exchange rate (TITN out per TGT in) must be monotonically non-increasing over time.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the quote rate increases over time, it breaks the incentive model designed to encourage early migration, allowing latecomers to extract more value than intended.
invariants.invariant_logic: quoteTitn(amount, t1) >= quoteTitn(amount, t2) for t1 <= t2
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_quote_decay(uint256 amount, uint256 t1, uint256 t2) public view { if(t1 <= t2) assert(quoteTitnAt(amount, t1) >= quoteTitnAt(amount, t2)); }

invariants.description: A user's proportional share of unclaimed TITN must not exceed the total unclaimed TITN.
invariants.attack_scenario: A math error (e.g., incorrect scaling or division by zero) could allow a user to drain more than their fair share of the remaining TITN pool, stealing from other depositors.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_proportional_share_bounds(address user) public view { uint256 unclaimed = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable; uint256 share = (claimableTitnPerUser[user] * unclaimed) / initialTotalClaimable; assert(share <= unclaimed); }

invariants.description: Once an address is marked as a bridged token holder, it cannot be unmarked (monotonicity of bridged status).
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can clear their bridged status, they can bypass the transfer restrictions intended for bridged tokens, violating cross-chain economic controls.
invariants.invariant_logic: isBridgedTokenHolder[user]_t1 == true => isBridgedTokenHolder[user]_t2 == true for t2 >= t1
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_bridged_status_monotonicity(address user) public view { assert(!wasBridged[user] || isBridgedTokenHolder[user]); }
